# agent-ssh

> A native macOS and iPadOS SSH workspace that folds the terminal, SFTP, a full PostgreSQL workspace, monitoring, and network tools into one window, running over a memory-safe Rust protocol core bridged to Swift with UniFFI.

## What it is

agent-ssh is the SSH workspace for people who live in production. It is a native macOS and iPadOS client where every panel is wired to the same Rust core through a typed event bus — no shelling out, no per-tool re-auth. The UI is native SwiftUI/AppKit; everything that touches the network is Rust, with generated, checksum-verified UniFFI bindings keeping the boundary honest. A single tokio runtime backs every feature, streaming PTY output, transfer progress, and monitor samples over one typed, back-pressured event bus. The protocol core, ssh-commander-core (SSH via russh, PostgreSQL, and Parquet), is published on crates.io.

## Who it's for

People who operate production systems and want the terminal, file transfer, database work, monitoring, and network diagnostics in one native window instead of juggling separate tools. It is also a good reference for engineers interested in cross-language FFI done safely, a single async runtime and event bus, and multi-platform apps built from one shared core.

## Key features

- Terminal: SwiftTerm PTY rendering full xterm-256color, with regex search, tabbed sessions, split panes, and a ⌘K command palette over connections and saved queries.
- Dual-pane transfer: side-by-side local/remote browsing, drag-and-drop, chmod, and a three-way diff with snapshot-before-write so a typo in nginx.conf never takes a host down.
- PostgreSQL workspace: multi-database schema tree, per-tab query cursors, click-to-edit cells, and cursor-paginated CSV / JSONL / Parquet export over an SSH tunnel.
- Network tools: multi-perspective DNS, a listening-port inventory with PID + process, streaming tcpdump, and remote git deploy-state — all over an existing session.
- Live monitoring: CPU, memory, disk, and load-average time series in SwiftUI Charts, plus a sortable remote process list with a kill action.
- Security model: secrets live in the Keychain only; host-key trust is TOFU and fails closed; on iPadOS, destructive actions sit behind a Face ID privacy gate.
- Runbooks and an iPadOS-first surface: repeatable command sequences with per-step recording and partial-failure triage; on iPad, a connection map by latency, an incident-report builder, SFTP that mounts into Files, and long tasks that survive backgrounding via BGTaskScheduler.

## Link

- Source and documentation: https://github.com/asmuelle/agent-ssh
